tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, bilirubin, ketones, and glucosuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine color/appearance, further supporting renal dysfunction. HbA1c is low, which may indicate hypoglycemia or a laboratory artifact. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or hepatic disorders.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
